description:
## Question

How is the reference's /superwhisper on|off|toggle skill expressed in this repo's declarative skill system?

The reference skill writes /tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5(pwd)> to disable per-project. Decide:
- Where it lives: home-modules/claude-skills.nix (this repo's curated skillset; note disableBundledSkills=true).
- Whether the flag actually gates hook behavior (depends on ticket 1's finding) or is purely a statusline display toggle — and whether that changes whether the skill is worth porting.
- The exact flag path/convention to standardize on, since the statusline indicator (ticket 5) must read the same convention.

HITL prototype/grilling.

comments.text:
RESOLVED (prototype/design). Artifact: home-modules/skills/superwhisper/SKILL.md (committed nix-4t3.4). Wired into voice.nix at build time (nix-4t3.6).

DESIGN:
- WORTH PORTING (settled by nix-4t3.1): the binary honors /tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5(cwd)> ('Exiting: superwhisper disabled for cwd='), so the flag gates REAL hook behavior, not just display.
- FLAG CONVENTION is DICTATED by the binary, not our choice: /tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5($PWD)>, hash = md5 of the session cwd (NOT git root). Both this skill and the nix-4t3.5 statusline MUST match it exactly. (Note: this is a case where matching the external tool overrides the repo's usual 'anchor to repo root' preference — documented in the SKILL.md.)
- AUTHORING: real repo file home-modules/skills/superwhisper/SKILL.md — faithful port of the reference one-liner (on=rm, off=touch, empty=toggle). Swapped 'echo -n' -> 'printf %s' for shell portability; identical hash.
- PLACEMENT: voice.nix sets user.home.file.'.claude/skills/superwhisper/SKILL.md'.source = the repo file, under mkIf voice.enable. Extends 'one module owns the integration' (cask+hooks+statusline+skill). Independence: gated on voice.enable alone; a stray skill file where claude-code is off is harmless.
- NAME: 'superwhisper' -> slash command /superwhisper [on|off]. Coexists with claude-skills.nix (same home.file mechanism, distinct name; no collision).

title: Design the per-project SuperWhisper toggle skill port
